title: Christian, seek not yet repose
publicDomainBasis: Charlotte Elliott (1789–1871), William Henry Monk (1823–1889). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Christian, seek not yet repose,
Hear thy gracious Savior say;
Thou art in the midst of foes:
Watch and pray.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Principalities and powers,
Mustering their unseen array,
Wait for thy unguarded hours:
Watch and pray.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Gird thy heavenly armor on,
Wear it ever night and day;
Ambushed lies the evil one:
Watch and pray.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Hear the victors who o’ercame,
Still they mark each warrior’s way;
All with one sweet voice exclaim,
Watch and pray.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
Hear, above all, hear thy Lord,
Him thou lovest to obey;
Hide within thy heart His word:
Watch and pray.

sections.label: Verse 6
sections.text:
Watch, as if on that alone
Hung the issue of the day;
Pray, that help may be sent down:
Watch and pray.
